title: Life as a Ger: Stories from Jewish Converts Around the World

sources.title: Book of Ruth
sources.relevance: Ruth's declaration - the foundational conversion story
sources.quote: Wherever you go, I will go; wherever you lodge, I will lodge; your people shall be my people, and your God my God.

sources.title: Talmud on Converts
sources.relevance: God scattered Israel among nations to increase converts
sources.quote: The Holy One, Blessed be He, exiled Israel among the nations only so that converts would join them

sources.title: Love for the Convert
sources.relevance: 36 times Torah commands to love the convert
sources.quote: The Torah warns against wronging a convert in thirty-six places

sources.title: Rambam on Conversion
sources.reference: Mishneh Torah, Laws of Forbidden Intercourse 13:14-17
sources.relevance: Requirements for conversion and status of ger
sources.quote: Once a convert immerses and accepts the mitzvot, they are like a Jew in every respect

sources.title: The Rebbe on Converts
sources.note: The Rebbe emphasized that converts bring down lofty souls

sources.title: Modern Conversion Stories
sources.note: Composite stories representing common conversion narratives
examples: Western converts discovering Judaism through study
Asian converts finding Judaism through Chabad
Intermarriage partners choosing to join the Jewish people
Spiritual seekers finding truth in Torah

key_themes: The special love Torah commands for converts

chinese_parallels.parallel: Chinese tradition of ritual adoption (认亲) mirrors joining the Jewish people
chinese_parallels.relevance: Both involve accepting new identity and obligations

chinese_parallels.parallel: Confucian relationships (师徒关系) similar to rav-student bond in conversion
Life-marking ceremonies in Chinese culture similar to mikvah immersion

notable_converts.name: Ruth the Moabite
notable_converts.period: Biblical era (~1100 BCE, Late Shang/Early Zhou Dynasty)
notable_converts.significance: Ancestor of King David and the Messiah

notable_converts.name: Onkelos
notable_converts.period: Talmudic era (1st-2nd century CE, Han Dynasty)
notable_converts.significance: Created the authoritative Aramaic translation of Torah

notable_converts.name: Rabbi Ovadia the Convert
notable_converts.significance: Corresponded with Rambam about his status as a ger
